revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:飽波神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Akunami Shrine | native_name = 飽波神社 | image = Akunami Jinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Akunami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.870008|138.257395}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} {{nihongo|'''Akunami Shrine'''|飽波神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suruga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province|ja|駿河国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11668489|1=List of Shikinaisha in Suruga Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Q134926697|ltq=Q134926697|qid=Q134926697|1=Miwa Shrine (Shizuoka)}} ** P156: {{ill|Nahe Shrine|lt=Nahe Shrine|ja|那閉神社|qid=Q28682737|1=Nahe Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|lt=Fujieda|ar|فوجيدا (شيزوكا)|arz|فوجيدا|azb|فوجی‌ئدا، شیزوئوکا|ceb|Fujieda|ce|Фудзиэда|cs|Fudžieda|de|Fujieda|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|envoyage|Fujieda|eo|Fuĝieda|es|Fujieda|et|Fujieda|fa|فوجی‌ئدا، شیزوئوکا|fi|Fujieda|fr|Fujieda|gl|Fujieda|id|Fujieda, Shizuoka|it|Fujieda|ja|藤枝市|javoyage|藤枝市|ko|후지에다시|mg|Fujieda|mzn|فوجی‌ئدا، شیزوئوکا|nl|Fujieda|no|Fujieda|pl|Fujieda|pt|Fujieda|ro|Fujieda|ru|Фудзиэда|sh|Fujieda|simple|Fujieda, Shizuoka|sr|Фуџиједа|sv|Fujieda|szl|Fujieda|tg|Фуҷиеда|th|ฟูจิเอดะ|tl|Fujieda|tr|Fujieda|tt|Фудзиэда|uk|Фудзієда (Сідзуока)|vi|Fujieda, Shizuoka|war|Fujieda, Shizuoka|zh_classical|藤枝市|zh_min_nan|Huzieda-chhī|zh_yue|藤枝|zh|藤枝市|zhvoyage|藤枝|qid=Q734432|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}} * {{ill|Q864170|ltq=Q864170|qid=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.870008|138.257395}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Akunami Jinja.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181548 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 426-0025https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7080005005633 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県藤枝市藤枝5-15-36', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:飽波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28682730}} == Japanese content == {{Source specified|date=2026-01}}{{Shrine |Name = Akunami Shrine |Image= [[File:Akunami Jinja.JPG|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|52|12.3|N|138|15|27|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fujieda City|Q734432|qid=Q734432|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}} Fujieda 5-15-36 |Latitude degrees = 34 |Latitude minutes = 52 |Latitude seconds = 12.3 |Longitude degrees = 138 |Longitude minutes = 15 |Longitude seconds = 27 |Map = Japan Shizuoka |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Sukuna-Bikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukuna-Bikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|qid=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}}
{{ill|lt=Hiruko|Q1067028|qid=Q1067028|lt=Hiruko-no-Mikoto|1=Hiruko}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
{{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Sonsha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Former Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = Year 6 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} ({{ill|lt=318|Q32369|qid=Q32369|1=318}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other name = Kawaseki Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Cultural properties = |Annual festival = First Saturday, Sunday, and Monday of October |Rituals = }} '''Akunami Shrine''' (あくなみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Fujieda 5-chōme, {{ill|lt=Fujieda City|Q734432|qid=Q734432|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. === Overview === [[File:Akunami jinja 1.jpg|thumb|200px|“Akunami Shrine Mikoshi Procession” around Taishō 10 (1921)*Photographic Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Fujieda*, Furusato no Omoide 75, by Yagi Yōkō and Nomoto Kan’ichi, Tosho Kankōkai, 1980 (Shōwa 55), held by the National Diet Library, accessed March 22, 2019.]] According to shrine tradition, Akunami Shrine was enshrined in the 6th year of Emperor Nintoku (318) as the guardian deity of Akunami-go (the entire Shida Plain). It is said that pure water once sprang forth from around small stones at the foot of the mountain and was efficacious for various illnesses, and the shrine was revered as Kawaseki Daimyōjin, a deity who protected people from flooding of the {{ill|lt=Seto River (Shizuoka Prefecture)|Q11566620|qid=Q11566620|lt=Seto River|1=Seto river}}. In addition to the annual festival, a grand festival (the Fujieda Grand Festival) is held every three years, bustling with fourteen festival floats (dashi). The sacred precincts of Akunami Shrine have been selected as one of Shizuoka Prefecture’s “Hometown Forests (Shrine Forests and Temple Forests)” Top 100. === History === [[File:Engishiki cropped Engishiki Vol 2 Flame 69.jpg|thumb|200px|Volume 9 of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, which records “Akunami Shrine”{{ill|lt=Atsuo Masamune|Q3297160|qid=Q3297160|1=Masamune Atsuo}} (ed.), *Engishiki*, Vol. 2, Nihon Koten Zenshū Kankōkai, 1929, p. 129.]] Founded in the 6th year of Emperor Nintoku (318). In the 3rd year of Eiroku (1560) during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan|Q1210114|qid=Q1210114|lt=Takeda forces|1=Takeda clan}} invaded and burned the shrine buildings and old records. However, the shrine priest Sone Hikohachi Sadaya safeguarded the sacred object, escaped the calamity, and built a small shrine to enshrine it. In the 5th year of Shōtoku (1715) in the Edo period, the shrine buildings were reconstructed. In 1873 (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Sonsha|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In 1907 (Meiji 40), it was designated as a shrine to receive offerings. In 1931 (Shōwa 6), the worship hall was constructed by parishioners and devotees, and ritual implements and komainu guardian statues were donated. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Sukuna-Bikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukuna-Bikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|lt=Seoritsuhime|Q13579836|qid=Q13579836|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Seoritsu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Hiruko|Q1067028|qid=Q1067028|lt=Hiruko-no-Mikoto|1=Hiruko}} * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|Q462555|qid=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}} === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === Two shrines are located within the precincts: * Kanayama Shrine * Nanatsumori Shrine === Japan Heritage === On June 19, 2020 (Reiwa 2), the “Dedication Dance of the Akunami Shrine Grand Festival,” together with a group of {{ill|lt=cultural properties|Q858308|qid=Q858308|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} around {{ill|lt=Fujieda-juku|Q3069558|qid=Q3069558|1=Fujieda-juku}}—including “{{ill|lt=Tanaka Castle|Q2970753|qid=Q2970753|1=Tanaka Castle}} and the Tanaka Castle Honmaru Turret (Historic Site: Tanaka Castle Ruins),” the “Eternal Pine of {{ill|lt=Daikeiji (Fujieda City)|Q43594322|qid=Q43594322|lt=Daikeiji Temple|1=Daikei-ji Temple (Fujieda)}},” “{{ill|lt=Seto no Somenmeshi|Q106702187|qid=Q106702187|1=Seto dyed rice}},” and the “Tōkaidō Pine Avenue (Kami-Aoshima Area)”—was recognized as one of the constituent cultural properties of the {{ill|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|Q1009519|qid=Q1009519|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}’s {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Cultural Property Protection System|1=UNKNOWN}} designation {{ill|lt=Japan Heritage|Q18382798|qid=Q18382798|1=Japan Heritage}}, under the story “The First ‘Travel Boom’ in Japan Sparked by Yaji-san and Kita-san: A Journey through Sunshū—A Guidebook to Tōkaidō Travel (Dōchūki) as Depicted in Comic Fiction and Ukiyo-e.”[https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story094/index.html STORY #094: The First ‘Travel Boom’ in Japan Sparked by Yaji-san and Kita-san, a Journey through Sunshū] (Japan Heritage Portal Site, Agency for Cultural Affairs)[https://www.city.fujieda.shizuoka.jp/soshiki/sports_bunka/kaidobunka/oshirase/14964.html Recognized as Japan Heritage in Fiscal Year Reiwa 2] (Fujieda City). === Notes === {{Footnote help}} === External links === * [https://akunami.amebaownd.com/ Akunami Shrine Official Website] {{Shinto}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Akunami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Akunami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Akunami Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujieda]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujieda]] [[Category:Japan Heritage]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]]
